seoTitle: Earlwood vs East Albury — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Earlwood and East Albury. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Earlwood vs East Albury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Earlwood and East Albury.
heading: Earlwood vs East Albury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Earlwood, New South Wales and East Albury, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Albury has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Earlwood (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Earlwood or East Albury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Earlwood has a flood risk score of 30/100, while East Albury scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Earlwood scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. East Albury scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Earlwood or East Albury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Albury has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
